title: subworkflow - dynatrace get recent problems with filters
description:
Subworkflow to retrieve recent Dynatrace problems with filtering options

This subworkflow retrieves recent problems with optional filtering by status, severity, and time range.

Parameters:

status: (Optional) Problem status filter (OPEN, RESOLVED, ALL) - default ALL
severityLevel: (Optional) Severity level filter (CRITICAL, MAJOR, MINOR, etc.)
timeFrameMinutes: (Optional) Look back time in minutes - default 60
maxProblems: (Optional) Maximum number of problems to return - default 50

Returns full problem details including impact analysis and affected entities
guide:
## 📊 **Subworkflow to Get Recent Problems with Filters**

🔍 This subworkflow retrieves recent Dynatrace problems with flexible filtering options for **status, severity, and time range**.

It enables drilling into problem data without requiring JavaScript knowledge, making it easy to build dashboards, reports, and custom automations.

---

### ⚙️ **Parameters**

- **status** (Optional): Problem status filter  
  *(Options: `OPEN`, `RESOLVED`, `ALL`) - Default: `ALL`*
- **severityLevel** (Optional): Severity level filter  
  *(Examples: `CRITICAL`, `MAJOR`, `MINOR`)*
- **timeFrameMinutes** (Optional): Look-back time in minutes  
  *(Default: `60`)*
- **maxProblems** (Optional): Maximum problems to return  
  *(Default: `50`)*

---

### 📈 **Returns**

- Problem ID, title, status
- Impact level and severity
- Affected entities and user count
- Root cause information
- Timestamps (start, resolution)

---

### 🧠 **Usage Context**

- ✅ Building problem dashboards
- ✅ Compliance reporting
- ✅ SLA tracking
- ✅ Trend analysis

---
